description:
You are acting as an infrastructure engineer who is preparing a very small-scale provisioning routine for a production machine.

Target state that MUST be reached
------------------------------------------------
1. Directory
   • /home/user/provisioning
     – If it does not exist, create it.

2. Environment file
   • /home/user/provisioning/prod.env
     – File permissions must be 600 (readable & writable only by the owner).
     – File content must be EXACTLY the two lines shown below, with a single trailing
       newline and **no extra blank lines or spaces** anywhere:

       API_TOKEN=prod-123456
       REGION=us-east-2

3. Audit / log file
   • /home/user/provisioning/setup.log
     – Must contain exactly **one** line.
     – Line format:  “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS prod.env configured”
       • Example: 2024-05-19 14:59:08 prod.env configured
     – The timestamp must be generated dynamically at run-time; the automated test
       only checks that it matches the pattern:
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} prod.env configured$

Execution constraints
------------------------------------------------
• Achieve the entire target state using **no more than three terminal commands**.
• You are working in a normal user shell; do not request sudo/root privileges.

Verification expectations
------------------------------------------------
The automated checker will confirm ALL of the following:
• Directory “/home/user/provisioning” exists and is a directory.
• File “/home/user/provisioning/prod.env” exists, is a regular file, has mode 600,
  and its contents match the two-line block shown above **exactly**.
• File “/home/user/provisioning/setup.log” exists, is a regular file, contains
  exactly one line, and that line matches the stated timestamp pattern.
• No extra files were created inside /home/user/provisioning.

Whenever you need to confirm success for yourself, you may print the file contents
or use ‘ls -l’, but remember that the automated grader only checks the filesystem
state described above.

Goal
------------------------------------------------
Create the directory and both files so they satisfy all the requirements listed.
truth:
Expected final state for grading
--------------------------------
Directory:
/home/user/provisioning      (exists; type=directory; permissions & ownership default user:user)

File #1:
/home/user/provisioning/prod.env
  • Content (exact text, newline-terminated):
    API_TOKEN=prod-123456
    REGION=us-east-2
  • Permissions: 600  (-rw-------)

File #2:
/home/user/provisioning/setup.log
  • Content must match regex:
    ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} prod.env configured\n?$
  • Exactly one line, newline optional but allowed.
  • Permissions: default (at least readable by owner).

No other files should exist inside /home/user/provisioning.
